raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding Citibanks mishandling and wrongful closure of a credit card dispute involving merchant fraud by XXXX. 

In late XX/XX/XXXX, I received the wrong item from the merchant XXXX. Between XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX confirmed in writing that they sent the wrong item, approved a full refund, and instructed me that I could keep the product. Despite this written confirmation, no refund was ever issued. 

XXXX later claimed the refund could take XXXX business days. I contacted Citi and, per Citis explicit instructions, I waited the full 53 business days before taking further action. No refund was posted. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, after the waiting period ended with no refund, I contacted Citi. I was instructed to email documentation and was told the dispute would be resolved. 

From XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Citi repeatedly. Multiple times I was told my dispute had not been reopened, then reopened again, and I was instructed to re-send the same documentation. On XX/XX/XXXX, I was told there was no record of the dispute and chat support could not assist me. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I again explained the full history and was told my documentation had been received and that I needed to wait an additional 3090 days for review. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank informed me that my dispute had been closed and denied because too much time had passed since opening. The passage of time was caused entirely by Citibanks own instructions, delays, and administrative failures, not by any inaction on my part. 

I followed Citibanks instructions at every step, relied on their guidance in good faith, and submitted all requested documentation multiple times. Denying my dispute due to elapsed time that Citibank itself caused is unfair and improper. 

This transaction qualifies as merchant fraud : the wrong item was delivered, the merchant admitted fault in writing, and no refund was ever issued.
